name: add-country-scoping-to-all-endpoints
description: Add check_country_access to every endpoint that accepts country parameter: itrp/router.py, buying/router.py (countries list filter), opco/router.py, schedule/router.py, reporting/router.py.
instruction:
For each router that has a country param:
1. Import check_country_access from auth.dependencies
2. After require_permission dependency returns user, call check_country_access(user, country)
3. For GET /api/buying/countries: filter response to user.allowed_countries() — if scoped, only return their countries
4. For GET /api/opcos?country: add check_country_access
5. For GET/PUT /api/schedule/{country_code}: add check_country_access
6. For reporting endpoints: add allowed_countries filter when data is country-specific

Files: modules/itrp/router.py, modules/buying/router.py, modules/opco/router.py, modules/schedule/router.py, modules/reporting/router.py
acceptance_criteria: OpCo user with scope_country=PL gets 403 when querying country=DE on any endpoint
GET /api/buying/countries returns only allowed countries for scoped users
Global users can access all countries without restriction

name: seed-mid-office-test-user
description: Add mid_office test user to seed data with scope_country=NL for testing country-scoped read access.
instruction: In seed.py DEMO_USERS: add {email: 'midoffice.nl@randstad.com', password: 'mid12345', full_name: 'Mid Office NL', role: 'mid_office', scope_country: 'NL'}. Add to seed function.
acceptance_criteria: midoffice.nl@randstad.com user exists in seed with mid_office role scoped to NL
Can login and see only NL data

name: verify-o004
description: Verify all endpoints have country scoping. Test with OpCo user.
instruction:
1. ruff check on all modified routers
2. Grep for check_country_access usage — should appear in every router with country param
3. Grep for endpoints WITHOUT any auth dependency — should be zero
4. Update O-004 KR
acceptance_criteria: Every endpoint with country parameter has check_country_access
